runs.name: Prototype a local Frankly OS v2 glass-box
runs.summary: Restored the standalone glaskasse prism material: chromatic edge light, spectral bands and RGB-split static refraction now make the background visibly bend through the glass. Frankly OS v2 remains de-integrated and clean.
runs.lesson: Review the standalone prism/refraction direction in outputs/prototypes/glass-box-system; only promote into any Frankly OS surface after Jonas explicit.

runs.name: Get the whole workspace to a clean green b
runs.summary: run-local-site-qa localRefs now exempts refs that carry an onerror graceful-degradation handler (the images-later pattern): the post ships before its art and hides the slot until the file lands, so a missing optional image no longer fails QA. Teeth preserved: a missing image WITHOUT onerror still fails (negative-tested). This was the only red in the workspace (journal posts 07-10, 16 optional images). Whole baseline now green.
runs.lesson: Jonas-only items remain: enable launchd, cockpit URL, Lab deploy, real journal images when ready.

runs.name: Reconcile the active-batch drift by regist
runs.summary: Registered MCQ-011 (Run Registry + Local Eval Harness) as a batch in mission-control-queue.json (evidenceType=report) and set activeBatch=MCQ-011 across queue/currentBatchId, machine-handoff-state, machine-core-priority, prompt-agent, system-live-data (2), the site feed, the mission-control-queue.html inline QUEUE and the handoff packet header. Evolved validate-machine-state.check_browser_evidence so non-UI batches prove themselves with their run report + artifacts (which must exist) instead of screenshots, and added check_v2_window_alignment so the v2 window can't drift from the core again. Machine-state PASS (0/0), evals 28/28. The active-batch drift is resolved; the daily packet + pulse now target MCQ-011.
runs.lesson: Jonas: add the 4 missing blog-07 images so site-QA localRefs goes green; then enable the launchd scheduler.

runs.name: Formally retire the three static OS dashbo
runs.summary: Retirement-in-place: deprecation banner (data-retired-banner) added to the 3 pages linking to the v2 window; dated register static-dashboard-retirement-2026-07-07.json; ledger + handoff updated. Kept files in place because 3 Control Cockpit evals read control-cockpit.html from disk and feed-sync/registry/nav still reference all three — hard removal scoped as a follow-up. Also fixed 2 dead blog/index.html nav links (fallout from the parallel blog->journal migration) by repointing to journal/frankly-blog-index.html, which had turned site-QA red independently of this work.
runs.lesson: Optional hard removal: retire cockpit evals + feed/registry refs + nav cross-links, then move files to outputs/legacy/.

runs.name: Make the system feel alive: heartbeat puls
runs.summary: self-check.py now computes active-batch consistency (catches MCQ-010 vs MCQ-011 drift) and writes it to the heartbeat feed; daily-run.py now also syncs site data feeds so the local data layer stays live. v2 Maskine renders a live pulse (green healthy / amber failing / red no-pulse when the job stops firing), a consistency warning, and polls every 60s so an always-open window stays current. Scheduler (launchd) is still Jonas's manual enable.
runs.lesson: Jonas enables the launchd job so the pulse beats daily; then reconcile the MCQ-010/011 drift (task_d5d14500).

runs.name: Surface the daily automation output (self-
runs.summary: self-check.py + daily-run.py now emit outputs/prototypes/frankly-os-site-v2/automation-status.json (health, checks, freshness, packet); Maskine renders it via paintAutomation. QA scanner does not scan v2, baseline stays green. Surfaced pre-existing drift: daily packet reads MCQ-010 from a 6-day-stale mission-control-queue.json while true active batch is MCQ-011.
runs.lesson: Fix mission-control-queue.

runs.name: Publish safe Frankly Lab online split afte
runs.summary: Updated the online os.franklydesign.dk bundle to the safe Frankly Test Lab boundary: gated landing plus BMW HTML test only; internal OS surfaces and product/legal drafts remain local. GitHub Actions deploy 28821748112 succeeded, then remote deploy workflow was returned to manual-only.
runs.lesson: Resolve git divergence between local frankly-os-site main and remote API commits before the next normal push; keep product/legal drafts out of public .

runs.name: Upgrade the local Frankly OS Slack lab so
runs.summary: Slack Chat Mesh v0.1 adds a first-class chat route, expanded specialist mesh, higher local run capacity and portable room contract while keeping Slack lab work local-only and approval-gated.
runs.lesson: Use chat: for local reusable multi-agent Slack lab rooms; keep live Slack/company-visible automation, Drive connector writes, paid APIs, plugin instal.

runs.name: Claude Run-to-Packet Launcher v0.1
runs.summary: Claude Run-to-Packet Launcher v0.1 upgrades Studio with a registry-fed source-run selector that pre-fills the Claude task packet with task shape, target surface, objective, source run and source lesson while preserving local gates.
artifacts: claude run to packet launcher v0 1
claude run to packet launcher v0 1
runs.lesson: A packet gets easier to trust when it starts from recorded machine memory instead of a blank prompt.

runs.name: Claude Task Packet Builder v0.1
runs.summary: Claude Task Packet Builder v0.1 upgrades Studio with a local read-only task packet builder for future Claude Code work, including task shape, target surface, objective, read-first files, allowed work, expected outputs, do-not-touch boundaries, validation commands, browser QA target, run trace and strict stop rule.
runs.lesson: A task packet becomes useful when the stop rules are copyable before the agent starts editing.

runs.name: Studio v0.1
runs.summary: Studio v0.1 adds a local noindex software-like Frankly OS workbench with command palette, mission/gate view, registry-fed run memory, validation console, Drive queue and Claude Code Execution Lane packet preview.
runs.lesson: The OS becomes easier to use when it feels like software, but still stays backed by files, gates and validation.

runs.name: Future Mode v0.1
runs.summary: Future Mode v0.1 previews likely next machine moves with lane, allowed work, blocked gates, validation and stop rule while keeping automation, connector writes and claims/product/legal locked.
runs.lesson: A future preview is useful only when it makes the stop rules as visible as the promise.

runs.name: Mission Theatre v0.1
runs.summary: Mission Theatre v0.1 turns recent local machine runs into brief, gate, build, validate and learn cues inside Observatory while using the same registry-fed run memory as Flight Recorder.
runs.lesson: Run memory becomes easier to use when the system can replay why it stopped.

runs.name: Launch Packet v0.1
runs.summary: Launch Packet v0.1 turns Mission Simulator output into a local agent-ready contract with mission, route, lane, do-not-touch boundaries, validation and stop rule while keeping connector writes and claims/product/legal locked.
runs.lesson: A visual preflight becomes more useful when it produces an agent-ready stop rule.

runs.name: Mission Simulator v0.1
runs.summary: Mission Simulator v0.1 previews future work inside Observatory as route, lane, allowed actions, blocked gates, validation path and output shape while keeping connector writes and claims/product/legal locked.
runs.lesson: Future work gets easier when gates are previewed before tools move.

runs.name: Drive-Ready Bundle v0.1
runs.summary: Drive-Ready Bundle Dry Run v0.1 verifies the six queued Drive continuity runs locally, generates a checksum-backed approval packet, updates continuity state, passes Observatory browser QA and writes a Monday 2026-07-06 decision brief without connector writes.
runs.lesson: Decision packets should be generated before connector writes are requested.

runs.name: Registry Feed v0.1
runs.summary: Registry Feed v0.1 makes Flight Recorder read frankly-os-site/data/flight-recorder.json generated from run-registry.json, with direct-file fallback preserved and all connector, deploy and claims gates closed.
runs.lesson: Use the registry-fed Flight Recorder as the default recent-run memory surface; pause unless Jonas approves Drive sync or asks for another concrete non.

runs.name: Flight Recorder v0.1
runs.summary: Flight Recorder v0.1 makes recent Frankly OS machine runs inspectable inside Observatory with replay controls, route/status panels, artifacts, gates, validation and reusable lessons while preserving all connector, deploy and claims gates.
runs.lesson: A replay surface should read the registry so memory does not drift.

runs.name: Observatory v0.1
runs.summary: Observatory v0.1 adds a local noindex machine constellation surface with canvas signal map, route forecast, Drive pending dock, run replay, gate radar and route-health lanes without touching claims/product/legal or connector writes.
runs.lesson: A vivid surface works best when grounded in real machine state.

runs.name: Drive Pending Queue
runs.summary: Drive Pending Queue rep 001 lists three completed-local Frankly OS runs awaiting explicit Drive connector approval and prepares target folders, artifact lists, shared snapshots, exclusions and validation rules without performing any connector writes.
runs.lesson: Connector-gated continuity should be a visible queue, not a memory burden.

runs.name: Route QA Two-Lane
runs.summary: Route QA Two-Lane Reporting rep 003 updated run-route-qa.py to preserve legacy Route QA fields while adding structural completeness and promotion readiness lanes; rep 003 shows 20 structurally complete routes, 12 ready-active, 3 gated-ready and 5 needing real pilots.
gates: no route promotion without real pi
runs.lesson: Use Route QA rep 003 as the route-health baseline; do not promote test-next or gated routes without a real pilot or explicit approval.

runs.name: Capability Radar
runs.summary: Capability Radar Pilot rep 001 used Control Cockpit, the Tool/Connector Guard Matrix and Route QA rep 002 to produce a local build/test/park/reject decision: pause unless Drive-sync housekeeping is explicitly approved or Jonas asks for a concrete non-claim deliverable.
runs.lesson: Pause after validation; if continuing, explicitly approve Drive-sync housekeeping for pending local runs or provide a concrete non-claim deliverable.

runs.name: Route Door Hardening
runs.summary: Route Quality Door Hardening rep 002 added explicit quality doors to seven gated/test-next route docs, produced a fresh route QA artifact with zero missing structural checks, and kept claims/product/legal scope locked.
runs.lesson: Use the structurally complete route layer for one small non-claim pilot, starting from Control Cockpit and the Tool/Connector Guard Matrix; Drive-sync.

runs.name: Control Cockpit v0.1
runs.summary: Control Cockpit v0.1 gives Jonas and future coding agents one internal launch surface for current state, active gates, Claude Code execution lane, run history, Drive continuity and next actions; artifacts are Drive-synced and claims scope remains locked.
runs.lesson: Use Control Cockpit v0.

runs.name: Tool Guard Matrix
runs.summary: Tool/Connector Guard Matrix classifies filesystem, git, browser, Drive, Slack, OpenAI API, deploy, plugin install, thread creation and claims/product/legal actions before more automation.
runs.lesson: Use the matrix as preflight on the next non-claim agent/loop/routes task, then record validation and Drive continuity.

runs.name: Repository Instructions
runs.summary: Repository Instructions rep 001 created root and site AGENTS.md files, a machine-readable artifact, a report, a Drive upload manifest and eval coverage without touching claims/product/legal scope.
runs.lesson: Repository Instructions rep is Drive-synced; close root/site git commits, then define a tool/connector guard matrix before more automation.

runs.name: Presentation Machine
runs.summary: Presentation Machine rep 001 produced a 12-slide internal Frankly OS deck spine with approval gates and no claims/product/legal scope touch.
runs.lesson: Presentation Machine rep is Drive-synced; keep the deck spine internal-only until visual QA and Jonas approval, and close root/site git commits before.

runs.name: Route QA rep 001
runs.summary: Route QA rep 001 audited 20 route docs, hardened four active routes and left 0 high-risk active route findings.
runs.lesson: Run Presentation Machine as MCQ-011 route rep 2/3 and create a Frankly OS deck spine without touching claims/product/legal scope.

runs.name: Run Registry v0.1
runs.summary: Install a local run registry, recorder script and eval coverage so future machine runs become traceable before more automation.
runs.lesson: The system can only learn from work it records.

runs.name: Claims Lock
runs.summary: Recorded Jonas's claims-work lock, redirected the active next move away from evidence/source work and added a local Frankly OS eval harness.
runs.lesson: A good machine must remember what not to work on.
